technologies.name: AWS
technologies.evidence: The architecture spans multiple AWS Regions and Availability Zones (AZs), leveraging AWS-managed services across all layers.
technologies.reasoning: The text explicitly mentions AWS as the cloud provider used for the MSSP Portal Cloud Operations Solution.

technologies.name: Amazon EKS
technologies.evidence: Application microservices run on an Amazon EKS Kubernetes cluster, with its control plane distributed across AZs for resilience.
technologies.reasoning: The text explicitly mentions Amazon EKS as the container orchestration service used.

technologies.name: Amazon RDS
technologies.evidence: Data persistence is managed by Amazon RDS in a Multi-AZ configuration, ensuring durability and availability.
technologies.reasoning: The text explicitly mentions Amazon RDS as the database service used.

technologies.name: AWS WAF
technologies.evidence: The platform is secured within a dedicated VPC, protected at the edge by AWS WAF against web-based threats.
technologies.reasoning: The text explicitly mentions AWS WAF as the web application firewall used.

technologies.name: Amazon Route 53
technologies.evidence: Amazon Route 53 delivers highly available DNS services, while Amazon CloudFront accelerates global web traffic, offloads origin servers, and enhances overall system reliability.
technologies.reasoning: The text explicitly mentions Amazon Route 53 as the DNS service used.

technologies.name: Amazon CloudFront
technologies.evidence: Amazon CloudFront accelerates global web traffic, offloads origin servers, and enhances overall system reliability.
technologies.reasoning: The text explicitly mentions Amazon CloudFront as the content delivery network used.

technologies.name: Terraform
technologies.evidence: All infrastructure and workloads are provisioned and managed as code. Terraform scripts and Kubernetes Helm charts automate the setup and configuration of cloud resources, guaranteeing consistency and repeatability in deployments.
technologies.reasoning: The text explicitly mentions Terraform as the infrastructure as code tool used.

technologies.name: Kubernetes Helm
technologies.evidence: Terraform scripts and Kubernetes Helm charts automate the setup and configuration of cloud resources, guaranteeing consistency and repeatability in deployments.
technologies.reasoning: The text explicitly mentions Kubernetes Helm as the package manager used.

technologies.name: Elasticsearch
technologies.evidence: The platform implements logs monitoring with Elasticsearch, Logstash, and Kibana (ELK stack) and metrics monitoring with Prometheus and Grafana, providing real-time insights, anomaly detection, and unified dashboards across all tenants.
technologies.reasoning: The text explicitly mentions Elasticsearch as part of the ELK stack used for observability.

technologies.name: Logstash
technologies.evidence: The platform implements logs monitoring with Elasticsearch, Logstash, and Kibana (ELK stack) and metrics monitoring with Prometheus and Grafana, providing real-time insights, anomaly detection, and unified dashboards across all tenants.
technologies.reasoning: The text explicitly mentions Logstash as part of the ELK stack used for observability.

technologies.name: Kibana
technologies.evidence: The platform implements logs monitoring with Elasticsearch, Logstash, and Kibana (ELK stack) and metrics monitoring with Prometheus and Grafana, providing real-time insights, anomaly detection, and unified dashboards across all tenants.
technologies.reasoning: The text explicitly mentions Kibana as part of the ELK stack used for observability.

technologies.name: Prometheus
technologies.evidence: The platform implements logs monitoring with Elasticsearch, Logstash, and Kibana (ELK stack) and metrics monitoring with Prometheus and Grafana, providing real-time insights, anomaly detection, and unified dashboards across all tenants.
technologies.reasoning: The text explicitly mentions Prometheus as the metrics monitoring tool used.

technologies.name: Grafana
technologies.evidence: A central Grafana server in a dedicated 'Tools' VPC is VPC-peered with all environments, providing unified dashboards aggregating metrics from all tenants.
technologies.reasoning: The text explicitly mentions Grafana as the visualization tool used.

technologies.name: Jenkins
technologies.evidence: Jenkins pipelines automate deployment of Terraform/Helm stacks on code merges.
technologies.reasoning: The text explicitly mentions Jenkins as the CI/CD tool used.

technologies.name: AWS Secrets Manager
technologies.evidence: AWS Secrets Manager stores deployment credentials and certificates securely.
technologies.reasoning: The text explicitly mentions AWS Secrets Manager as the secrets management tool used.
